response:
The equilibrium population size occurs when the rate of change is zero, meaning the growth exactly balances the harvesting loss. For the first scenario with constant parameters, we set the derivative to zero and solve for P using the given values of r equal to 0.1 and K equal to 1000. The harvesting function h(t) oscillates between 5 and 15, so a single constant equilibrium P_e does not exist in the strict sense because the right side of the equation varies with time. However, if we seek the equilibrium corresponding to the average harvesting rate, we calculate the average of h(t) as 10. Solving 0.1 times P times (1 minus P over 1000) equals 10 yields a quadratic equation where P equals 100 or P equals 900. The stable equilibrium is the larger value, which is 900 turtles, while the smaller value represents an unstable threshold.

When the carrying capacity decreases linearly as K(t) equals 1000 minus 3t, the system becomes non-autonomous and the equilibrium point shifts continuously. To find the time when the population is no longer sustainable, we look for the moment when the maximum possible stable equilibrium drops to zero or below. This happens when the carrying capacity itself becomes so low that even with zero harvesting the population cannot persist, or more precisely, when the discriminant of the equilibrium equation becomes negative or the positive root vanishes. Setting the growth term to equal the maximum possible harvest to find the tipping point is complex, but a simpler sustainability limit occurs when the carrying capacity K(t) falls below the minimum required to support the population against the minimum harvest. If we assume the population tracks the stable equilibrium, it becomes unsustainable when the equation 0.1 times P times (1 minus P over K(t)) equals h(t) has no positive real solution. This occurs when the peak of the logistic growth curve, which is r times K(t) over 4, falls below the maximum harvest rate. The maximum harvest is 15. So we set 0.1 times (1000 minus 3t) divided by 4 equal to 15. Solving this gives 1000 minus 3t equals 600, which means 3t equals 400, so t equals approximately 133.3 years. At this point the population collapses. A palindrome to remember this critical time is
